steps.title: Identify the key signal
steps.content: The passage establishes that Indigenous knowledge documented in songs produces measurable ecological results: archaeological and paleoecological records confirm the clam gardens described in Kwakwaka'wakw songs actually increased clam size and productivity.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content: If songs from the Kwakwaka'wakw people contain accurate, useful ecological knowledge, researchers logically should consider whether songs from other Indigenous peoples might also contain such information—exactly what choice B proposes. The alternatives either deny the people's documented ecological impact (A), mistrust the evidence that proved the songs' accuracy (C), or raise questions the finding never addresses (D). That matches choice B — B is correct.
